title: Fixing GIT Ref Name Validation with Forbidden Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a GIT reference name. The name should not contain any forbidden characters. Forbidden characters are '/', '\', and '\n'. The function should return true if the name is valid, and false otherwise. The function should be case-insensitive.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the GIT reference name.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the name is valid or not.
statement.notes: The function should handle edge cases such as empty strings and names containing only forbidden characters.

constraints.description: The length of the input string

examples.explanation: The name '/dev/null' contains a forbidden character '/'.
The name '\n' contains a forbidden character '\n'.

hints.text: Check if the input string contains any forbidden characters.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing GIT Ref Name Validation with Forbidden Characters
editorial.intuition: The function should check if the input string contains any forbidden characters. It should handle edge cases such as empty strings and names containing only forbidden characters.
editorial.bruteForce: The function can use a loop to check each character in the input string. It can use the `indexOf` method to check if the character is in the forbidden characters string.
editorial.optimalSolution: The function can use a regular expression to check if the input string contains any forbidden characters. It can use the `test` method to check if the regular expression matches the input string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function returns true if the input string does not contain any forbidden characters, and false otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: The function can be tested with the example inputs provided in the statement.
